title: My New Small Habit
summary: Explain a small new habit you started recently, using present simple and past simple.
topic: A small new habit I started
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about one small new habit you started in the last few months. Say what the habit is, when you do it, and why you started. Use present simple for the habit and past simple for when you started it.
hintStarters: A few months ago, I started…
Now I always do this because…
At first it was hard, but…
sampleText: A few months ago, I started a small new habit. Every morning, I drink one full glass of water before I eat or drink anything else. I started this because I usually felt tired in the mornings. A friend told me about it and I decided to try. At first, I sometimes forgot. I put a glass on my desk the night before to remember. After a week, it became easier. Now I always do it without thinking. I never skip this habit because it only takes one minute. I notice that I feel less tired in the first hour of my day. I do not know if the water is the reason but I think it helps. My body feels a little better and I start the day in a calm way. It is a very small change but it is easy to keep. I sometimes tell friends about it because it is free and takes almost no time.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: before I eat or drink anything else
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: trước khi ăn hay uống bất cứ thứ gì khác

sampleAnnotations.phrase: usually felt tired in the mornings
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: thường cảm thấy mệt vào buổi sáng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: put a glass on my desk the night before
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đặt một cái ly trên bàn từ tối hôm trước

sampleAnnotations.phrase: after a week, it became easier
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: sau một tuần, việc đó trở nên dễ hơn

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Mixing present simple and past simple
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng quá khứ đơn để kể lúc bắt đầu: 'I started', 'I decided', 'I forgot'. Dùng hiện tại đơn cho thói quen đang làm bây giờ: 'I drink', 'I always do it', 'I never skip'. Sự kết hợp này giúp cho câu chuyện rõ ràng: quá khứ → bây giờ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past simple for when the habit started: 'I started', 'I decided', 'I forgot'. Use present simple for what you do now: 'I drink', 'I always do it', 'I never skip'. This mix shows: past beginning → present routine.

sampleTranslationVi: Vài tháng trước, tôi bắt đầu một thói quen nhỏ mới. Mỗi sáng, tôi uống một ly nước đầy trước khi ăn hay uống bất cứ thứ gì khác. Tôi bắt đầu làm điều này vì tôi thường cảm thấy mệt vào buổi sáng. Một người bạn kể cho tôi nghe và tôi quyết định thử. Lúc đầu, đôi khi tôi hay quên. Tôi đặt một cái ly trên bàn từ tối hôm trước để nhớ. Sau một tuần, việc đó trở nên dễ hơn. Bây giờ tôi luôn làm điều đó mà không cần phải nghĩ. Tôi không bao giờ bỏ thói quen này vì nó chỉ mất một phút. Tôi nhận thấy mình ít mệt hơn trong giờ đầu tiên của ngày. Tôi không biết liệu nước có phải là nguyên nhân không nhưng tôi nghĩ nó có giúp ích. Cơ thể tôi cảm thấy tốt hơn một chút và tôi bắt đầu ngày mới theo cách bình tĩnh hơn. Đây là một thay đổi rất nhỏ nhưng dễ duy trì. Đôi khi tôi kể cho bạn bè nghe vì nó miễn phí và hầu như không tốn thời gian.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses past simple to say when a habit started?
options: A few months ago, I start this habit.
A few months ago, I started this habit.
A few months ago, I starting this habit.
A few months ago, I am starting this habit.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'started' is correct for a finished action in the past.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the correct past simple form of 'start'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses present simple to describe a current habit?
options: Every morning I drank one glass of water.
Every morning I am drinking one glass of water.
Every morning I drink one glass of water.
Every morning I will drink one glass of water.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple 'I drink' with 'every morning' shows a repeated current habit.
mcQuestions.hint: Which tense describes something you do every day as a habit?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'because' to explain why a habit started?
options: I started it because I felt tired.
I started it because felt tired.
I started it because to feel tired.
Because tired I started it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because I felt tired' is a complete clause with subject + verb.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because', you need a subject and a verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' to show the contrast between a hard start and an easy now?
options: At first it was hard and now it is easy.
At first it was hard because now it is easy.
At first it was hard but now it is easy.
At first it was hard so now it is easy.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' is the right connector to contrast 'hard at first' with 'easy now'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which connector shows two situations that are opposite?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'never' with a reason?
options: I never skip this habit because it is fast.
I skip never this habit because it is fast.
I never skip this habit because fast.
Never I skip this habit because it is fast.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'never' goes before the main verb 'skip': 'I never skip'. 'because' is followed by a full clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Check both the position of 'never' and the structure after 'because'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple form: 'My friend ___ me about it.'
options: tell
telled
tells
told
mcQuestions.explanation: 'tell' is an irregular verb — the past simple form is 'told'.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the irregular past form of 'tell'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes a way to remember a new habit?
options: I put a glass on my desk the night before.
I put a glass on my desk last year.
I putting a glass on my desk every night.
I putted a glass on my desk the night before.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'put' in past simple is 'put' — an irregular verb with the same base and past form. 'the night before' gives the time frame.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past simple form of 'put'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase correctly uses 'after' to show a time change?
options: After a week, it becomes easier.
After a week, it became easier.
After a week, it is becoming easier.
After a week, it will become easier.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'became' is correct — the change happened after a week, and is now finished.
mcQuestions.hint: Which tense shows a change that happened and finished in the past?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'always' with present simple?
options: Now I always do it without thinking.
Now I do always it without thinking.
Now always I do it without thinking.
Now I do it always without thinking.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'always' goes between the subject and the main verb: 'I always do'.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'always' go in a present simple sentence?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes noticing a small positive change?
options: I notice that I feel less tired in the morning.
I noticing that I feel less tired in the morning.
I noticed that I feel less tired in the morning always.
I am noticing that I felt less tired in the morning.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple 'I notice' + 'I feel' — both describe a current ongoing observation.
mcQuestions.hint: Both verbs should be in the same tense for a current observation.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means it does not cost any money?
options: it is expensive
it is free
it is cheap
it is paid
mcQuestions.explanation: 'it is free' means there is no cost — the most direct and natural way to say this.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word means something costs nothing?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Think of one small change you could make to your morning or evening that would take less than two minutes. Would you try it for one week to see what happens?
